rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical [3+2] cycloaddition of N-sulfonyl cyclopropylamines with alkenes/alkynes (cinnamates) to give aminocyclopentane/cyclopentene products via ring-opening of an aminyl radical, mediated by ferrocene.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Cp2Fe to Cp2Fe+ which oxidizes the deprotonated sulfonamide anion (A) to an N-centered aminyl radical (B); the distal alkyl radical adduct is reduced at the cathode to a carbanion that cyclizes/protonates.

key_intermediates.name: Sulfonamide anion (A) from K2CO3 deprotonation of N-sulfonylcyclopropylamine
Ring-opened distal carbon radical / N-sulfonyl iminyl-tethered alkyl radical (C)
Alkene/alkyne radical adduct (D) after addition to cinnamate (Michael acceptor)
5-exo cyclized cyclopentene carbanion (F/G) bearing N-Ts iminyl/amide
Cyclic N-sulfonyl imine intermediate III (detected by HRMS)
Hydrolysis product IV: tosylsulfonamide + aldehyde V (confirmed by 1H NMR)

electron_flow: K2CO3 deprotonates the N-H sulfonamide; ferrocene is oxidized at the anode to Cp2Fe+ which single-electron oxidizes the sulfonamide anion to an N-centered radical that undergoes cyclopropane ring-opening to a distal C-radical; this radical adds to the cinnamate, the resulting α-ester radical is reduced at the cathode to a carbanion which undergoes 5-exo cyclization onto the N-sulfonyl imine and protonation to give the trans-aminocyclopentane.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Complete shutdown of reaction, supporting a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Complete shutdown of reaction, supporting a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: N-acetyl cyclopropylamine (vs N-tosyl)
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction; tosyl (electron-withdrawing/stabilizing) group required, acetyl detrimental

probe_reagents.name: Divided cell experiment
probe_reagents.observation: ~70% starting material recovered; indicates no chain propagation

probe_reagents.name: (3-bromoprop-1-en-2-yl)benzene (2o) intermediate trap
probe_reagents.observation: HRMS detects cyclic N-sulfonyl imine III; hydrolysis gives tosylsulfonamide IV (NMR) + aldehyde V, consistent with an anionic 5-exo cyclization pathway

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry (Cp2Fe, 1a, 2a, K2CO3 combinations)
probe_reagents.observation: Establishes Cp2Fe as the redox mediator oxidizing the sulfonamide anion rather than direct substrate oxidation

notes: Authors propose two mechanisms: (favored, H) anionic 5-exo cyclization pathway via cathodic reduction of the radical adduct to a carbanion that cyclizes onto the N-sulfonyl imine (supported by HRMS detection of imine III and isolation of IV/V); (alternate, I) all-radical pathway where the alkene radical adduct cyclizes to a C-centered radical (J) which is cathodically reduced to a carbanion (K) and protonated. NOESY confirms trans diastereoselectivity, rationalized by steric repulsion between N-sulfonyl imine and ester in the chair-like transition state (Aggarwal model). Divided cell shows no radical chain propagation, consistent with stoichiometric mediator turnover.
